texto: Friendship isn't partying with a group of people to get drunk or chatting with him/her once a week, it's exactly the opposite. Friends make sure you get home safely and they help you when you need it, no matter the scenario. They don't care about what clothes you wear or what you look like, and they don't last for a day. Real friends are more interested in what direction your life is headed rather than your popularity. They care about what you have to say and how you feel, and once you meet this person you'll know it without having to think twice.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Morgan Tang: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Friendship isn't partying with a group of people to get drunk or chatting with him/her once a wee…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, drunk, friends pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e friends.

They care about what you have to say and how you feel, and once you meet this person you'll know it without having to think twice.
